source_paper_title: Fast Convex Optimization via Time Scale and Averaging of the Steepest Descent
source_paper_bibtex: @article{Attouch_2025, title={Fast Convex Optimization via Time Scale and Averaging of the Steepest Descent}, volume={50}, ISSN={1526-5471}, url={http://dx.doi.org/10.1287/moor.2023.0186}, DOI={10.1287/moor.2023.0186}, number={4}, journal={Mathematics of Operations Research}, publisher={Institute for Operations Research and the Management Sciences (INFORMS)}, author={Attouch, Hedy and Ioan Boţ, Radu and Nguyen, Dang-Khoa}, year={2025}, month=nov, pages={2633–2665} }
context:
Page 26, Section 5.1 (Remark 8).

It would be interesting to know if we can achieve the same convergence rate using time scaling and perturbation techniques.
title: Achieve faster residual decay for cocoercive-operator Newton-corrected inertial dynamics
background_latex:
Let \(H\) be a real Hilbert space with inner product \(\langle \cdot,\cdot\rangle\) and norm \(\|\cdot\|\). Let \(M:H\to H\) be a single-valued \(\rho\)-cocoercive operator for some \(\rho>0\), meaning
\[
\langle M(x)-M(y),x-y\rangle \ge \rho\,\|M(x)-M(y)\|^2\quad\forall x,y\in H.
\]
Assume the set of zeros \(\mathrm{Zer}
M:=\{x\in H: M(x)=0\}\) is nonempty.

For a trajectory \(y:[s_0,\infty)\to H\) with \(s_0>0\), define the (formal) time derivative of \(M(y(s))\) by the chain rule notation
\[
\frac{d}{ds}(M(y(s)))
\]
(when \(M\) is Lipschitz, this can be understood in the a.e. sense along absolutely continuous \(y\)).

Consider the second-order inertial dynamics with vanishing viscous damping and a Newton-correction term
\[
 y''(s)+\frac{\alpha}{s}y'(s)+c(s)\,\frac{d}{ds}(M(y(s)))+M(y(s))=0,\qquad s\ge s_0,
\]
where \(\alpha>1\) and \(c(s)>0\) is a prescribed scalar coefficient. Two specific choices discussed are
1) \(c(s)=\frac{s}{\alpha+1}\), yielding the system
\[
 y''(s)+\frac{\alpha}{s}y'(s)+\frac{s}{\alpha+1}\,\frac{d}{ds}(M(y(s)))+M(y(s))=0,
\]
for which the paper proves \(\|M(y(s))\|=o(1/s)\).
2) \(c(s)=\frac{2s}{\alpha+1}\), yielding the closely related system
\[
 y''(s)+\frac{\alpha}{s}y'(s)+\frac{2s}{\alpha+1}\,\frac{d}{ds}(M(y(s)))+M(y(s))=0,
\]
which another work reports satisfies the sharper decay \(\|M(y(s))\|=o(1/s^2)\) and \(\int_{s_0}^\infty s^3\|M(y(s))\|^2\,ds<\infty\).
problem_definition_latex:
Determine whether the time-scaling and perturbation approach developed for steepest-descent-type systems can be used to obtain the improved convergence rate
\[
\|M(y(s))\|=o\!\left(\frac{1}{s^2}\right)\quad\text{as }s\to\infty
\]
(and/or the corresponding weighted integrability \(\int_{s_0}^\infty s^3\|M(y(s))\|^2\,ds<\infty\)) for the Newton-corrected cocoercive-operator inertial dynamics
\[
 y''(s)+\frac{\alpha}{s}y'(s)+\frac{s}{\alpha+1}\,\frac{d}{ds}(M(y(s)))+M(y(s))=0,
\]
or for an appropriately modified coefficient \(c(s)\) within the same time-scaling/perturbation framework, under the standing assumptions that \(M\) is cocoercive and \(\mathrm{Zer}\,M\neq\emptyset\).

open_problem_quote: It would be interesting to know if we can achieve the same convergence rate using time scaling and perturbation techniques.
counterexample_suitability_rationale: The question asks for establishing an improved asymptotic rate under broad operator assumptions rather than refuting a concrete conjectured bound. Searching for counterexamples would require exploring infinite-dimensional operator classes and verifying asymptotic rates of ODE solutions, which is not a finite, well-parameterized instance space.
alphaevolve_suitability_rationale: An evolutionary code loop could explore alternative time scalings or coefficient functions \(c(s)\) and test empirically on finite-dimensional examples (e.g., linear cocoercive operators) by numerically integrating the ODE and measuring residual decay. This would not prove the general result but could discover promising scalings/perturbations that exhibit \(1/s^2\) decay and guide analysis.

verification.notice: Generated from the paper text only and not independently verified as still open.

literature_review.synthesis:
The source paper (Attouch–Bot–Nguyen) develops a general mechanism—time scaling of a first-order dissipative flow, followed by an averaging step and (when needed) an asymptotically vanishing perturbation argument—to obtain higher-order inertial dynamics with provable rates without constructing new Lyapunov functions. In the cocoercive-operator setting, starting from the basic flow \(z'(t)+M(z(t))=0\) (for \(\rho\)-cocoercive \(M\)), the framework yields the Newton-corrected inertial system with coefficient \(c(s)=\tfrac{s}{\alpha+1}\) and establishes residual decay \(\|M(y(s))\|=o(1/s)\) together with \(\int_{s_0}^\infty s\,\|M(y(s))\|^2\,ds<\infty\) for \(\alpha>1\). However, the method as implemented there does not reach the accelerated \(o(1/s^2)\) residual regime.

A closely related dynamics with a larger Newton-correction coefficient, \(c(s)=\tfrac{2s}{\alpha+1}\), is analyzed in Bot–Csetnek–Nguyen (Fast OGDA in continuous and discrete time), where one obtains the sharper estimates \(\|M(y(s))\|=o(1/s^2)\) and \(\int_{s_0}^\infty s^3\|M(y(s))\|^2\,ds<\infty\). This demonstrates that the accelerated residual decay is achievable for cocoercive operators, but via a different (or at least differently tuned) continuous-time model and analysis.

At present, with no forward-citation evidence provided beyond these works, the specific question remains open: whether the time-scaling/perturbation approach can be refined to recover \(o(1/s^2)\) for the \(c(s)=\tfrac{s}{\alpha+1}\) system, or whether one must modify the coefficient within that framework (e.g., effectively reproducing the \(2s/(\alpha+1)\) scaling or introducing additional averaging/regularization) to access the stronger weighted energy estimate \(\int s^3\|M(y(s))\|^2<\infty\). Promising directions suggested by the existing proofs include identifying a time scale \(\tau\) and perturbation \(g\) that upgrade the base residual bound for \(z'(t)+M(z(t))=g(t)\) from \(o(t^{-1/2})\) to \(o(t^{-1})\) (or better), since the current scaling \(\tau(s)\asymp s^2\) converts \(o(t^{-1/2})\) exactly into \(o(s^{-1})\); achieving \(o(s^{-2})\) would require a fundamentally stronger decay in the base flow or a different mechanism than simple time scaling.

sources.title: Fast convex optimization via time scale and averaging of the steepest descent
sources.theorem_or_result: Develops a time-scaling + averaging + perturbation framework that transfers convergence properties of the first-order steepest descent (and its perturbed version) to higher-order inertial dynamics. In the cocoercive-operator setting, derives for the Newton-corrected system with coefficient \(c(s)=\tfrac{s}{\alpha+1}\) the residual decay \(\|M(y(s))\|=o(1/s)\) and weighted integrability \(\int_{s_0}^\infty s\,\|M(y(s))\|^2\,ds<\infty\) for \(\alpha>1\).
sources.connection: This is the source paper posing the open question: whether the same time-scaling/perturbation approach can yield the sharper residual decay \(\|M(y(s))\|=o(1/s^2)\) known for a closely related dynamics with a larger Newton-correction coefficient.

sources.title: Fast OGDA in continuous and discrete time
sources.theorem_or_result: Studies the cocoercive-operator inertial dynamics \(y''(s)+\frac{\alpha}{s}y'(s)+\frac{2s}{\alpha+1}\frac{d}{ds}(M(y(s)))+M(y(s))=0\) and proves the sharper residual estimates \(\|M(y(s))\|=o(1/s^2)\) and \(\int_{s_0}^\infty s^3\|M(y(s))\|^2\,ds<\infty\) (under \(\alpha>1\) in the cited remark).
sources.connection: Provides the benchmark \(o(1/s^2)\) residual decay for the modified Newton-correction coefficient \(c(s)=\tfrac{2s}{\alpha+1}\). The open problem asks whether the time-scaling/perturbation framework can recover this rate (or similar) for \(c(s)=\tfrac{s}{\alpha+1}\) or via an admissible modification.

sources.title: A dynamical system associated with the fixed points set of a nonexpansive operator
sources.theorem_or_result: Establishes weak convergence for trajectories of first-order dynamics governed by nonexpansive operators and provides residual/velocity integrability estimates that are typical in cocoercive/nonexpansive settings.
sources.connection: Supplies foundational continuous-time convergence tools for cocoercive/nonexpansive operator flows that underlie the time-scaling approach when starting from \(z'(t)+M(z(t))=0\).

sources.title: First-order algorithms via inertial systems with hessian driven damping
sources.theorem_or_result: Develops Lyapunov analyses for inertial dynamics with Hessian-driven damping (explicit and implicit forms), proving fast \(O(1/s^2)\)/\(o(1/s^2)\) rates for function values and related integrability properties under suitable parameter regimes.
sources.connection: Although focused on gradient-driven dynamics, it provides methodological and structural analogues for Newton-corrected terms and suggests what kinds of weighted estimates (e.g., \(\int s^3\|\cdot\|^2\)) are plausible in accelerated regimes.

sources.title: Fast convex optimization via inertial dynamics with Hessian driven damping
sources.theorem_or_result: Introduces and analyzes inertial gradient dynamics with vanishing damping and Hessian-driven damping, establishing fast convergence rates and motivating Newton/Hessian correction terms to control oscillations.
sources.connection: Serves as a conceptual precursor for Newton-corrected inertial dynamics; while not in the general cocoercive-operator setting, it motivates the form of correction terms and the target accelerated decay rates.
